Buyer perception of 3D-printed instruments is mixed — audio-visual proof of sound quality (a short video or audio clip in listing) is essential to overcome scepticism about plastic instruments.

etsy_ban_reason: The scraped page references a membership-based commercial licensing system ('If you would like to sell prints of my models, you can obtain commercial licenses by joining a membership') — this means a paid commercial licence is required before selling prints on Etsy; the file is not freely licensed for commercial use, so verify the membership terms on the MakerWorld model page and obtain the appropriate licence before listing.
legal_desc: Because you are targeting worldwide sales including the US and EU, this product — a mouthpiece instrument placed in the mouth — may be classified as a toy or novelty musical item subject to CPSC/CPSIA (ASTM F963-23) in the US and EN 71 toy safety standards in the EU; you should confirm whether your target buyer demographic includes children and, if so, obtain the relevant third-party safety certification or restrict sales explicitly to adults.
copyright_desc: The 'Howling Baby Wolf' appears to be an original design by H2Jack Concepts with no identifiable third-party IP — no matching Disney, Nintendo, or other major IP holder detected.
